it's something with MDS-OST negotiation:

00000004:00001000:1.0:1729275290.308161:0:56391:0:(osp_dev.c:808:osp_statfs()) lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000: blocks=35554, bfree=3615, bavail=100, bsize=4096, reserved_mb_low=1, reserved_mb_high=3, files=50000, ffree=49084, state=20
00000004:00000001:1.0:1729275290.308163:0:56391:0:(osp_dev.c:831:osp_statfs()) Process leaving (rc=0 : 0 : 0)
00020000:01000000:1.0:1729275290.308164:0:56391:0:(lod_qos.c:107:lod_statfs_and_check()) lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000: turns inactive
00020000:00000001:1.0:1729275290.308165:0:56391:0:(lod_qos.c:135:lod_statfs_and_check()) Process leaving (rc=18446744073709551588 : -28 : ffffffffffffffe4)

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

The bad part of wait_delete_completed() is that it has a hard sleep in it, which would make any iteration much slower.  Maybe that should only be checked if free space is <50%?

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> The bad part of wait_delete_completed() is that it has a hard sleep in it, which would make any iteration much slower.  Maybe that should only be checked if free space is <50%?

right, it can be quite time consuming. do you mean to skip the test if free space < less than 50% ?

Patch Set 5:

Trevis Autotest 2 retesting enforced custom session on el9.3-x86_64/ldiskfs servers and clients for build 108414 patch 5. Requested by Alex Zhuravlev because of LU-18379.
The following test parameters were specified: env=ONLY="127",ONLY_REPEAT="20" testlist=conf-sanity.

Patch Set 5:

> do you mean to skip the test if free space less than 50% ?

It should skip calling wait_delete_completed() if free space > 50%.  In most cases (normal test run, or first few iterations of looping) then it won't add any overhead.   If there is less free space than 50% (eg. deletes accumulating), it is probably enough to call wait_delete_completed() once and then the free space will grow again.

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> > do you mean to skip the test if free space less than 50% ?
> 
> It should skip calling wait_delete_completed() if free space > 50%.  In most cases (normal test run, or first few iterations of looping) then it won't add any overhead.   If there is less free space than 50% (eg. deletes accumulating), it is probably enough to call wait_delete_completed() once and then the free space will grow again.

actually wait_delete_completed() is not that dumb :)
it checks for pending updates and returns immediately if can't find such

	# find MDS with pending deletions
	for node in $(mdts_nodes); do
		changes=$(do_node $node "$LCTL get_param -n osc.*MDT*.sync_*" \
			2>/dev/null | calc_sum)
		if [[ $changes -eq 0 ]]; then
			continue
		fi
		mds2sync="$mds2sync $node"
	done
	if [ -z "$mds2sync" ]; then
		wait_zfs_commit $SINGLEMDS
		return 0
	fi

commit.subject: LU-18379 tests: wait for stats to expire in conf-sanity/127
commit.message:
LU-18379 tests: wait for stats to expire in conf-sanity/127

fs stats are not updated immediately on the client,
thus we need to wait.

Test-Parameters: env=ONLY=127,ONLY_REPEAT=20 testlist=conf-sanity
Test-Parameters: env=ONLY=127,ONLY_REPEAT=20 testlist=conf-sanity
